Change of registered office address
Mirriad Advertising plc (AIM: MIRI), a leading virtual in-content advertising and virtual product placement company, announces that its registered office address has changed from 6th Floor One London Wall, London, England, EC2Y 5EB to 201 Temple Chambers, 3-7 Temple Avenue, London EC4Y 0DT with immediate effect.

About Mirriad
The leader in virtual product placement and in-content advertising, Mirriad's multi-patented and award-winning platform can dynamically insert products and brands into Television, SVOD/AVOD, Music, and Influencer content. Mirriad creates net-new revenue opportunities for content owners with an ad format that virtually integrates brands in entertainment content, drives exceptional performance for advertisers and dramatically improves the viewing experience.
Mirriad currently operates in: EMEA, the US (via a Joint Venture with Rembrand), and India.

latest.title: Trading Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 5512Y
Mirriad Advertising PLC
30 March 2026
30 March 2026
Mirriad Advertising plc
("
Mirriad
" or the "
Company
")
Trading Update
Mirriad, a leading provider of virtual product placement ("
VPP
"), provides a trading update on the first quarter of 2026.
The Company's trading update, announced on 14 January 2026, noted that it was cautiously optimistic about Mirriad's outlook and that it was optimistic for a potentially significant uptick in sales during February and March. This was in part due to expectations discussed with partners and a seasonal sales increase expected during Ramadan in the Middle East. However, geopolitical events, principally the conflict in Iran, regrettably had a major impact on sales during the period, meaning this did not materialize as anticipated.
The Company is pleased to announce that during the quarter it entered into a services agreement (the "
Agreement
") with one of the UK's largest media conglomerates. Under the terms of the Agreement, Mirriad will provide the company with its VPP solution and try to help them scale incremental revenue through utilizing Mirriad's VPP solution. The client is currently in market to brands for a go-to-market test campaign.
As at 27 March 2026, the Company had cash and cash equivalents of c. £675k. Costs continue to be carefully managed, however it is anticipated that the Company will be required to secure further funding prior to the publication of its annual report and accounts for the year ended 31 December 2025. Further updates will be made at the appropriate time.
For further information please visit
www.mirriad.com
or contact:

latest.title: Full year trading update
latest.content:
14 Jan 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 7785O
Mirriad Advertising PLC
14 January 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF ARTICLE 7 OF THE MARKET ABUSE REGULATION (596/2014/EU) AS IT FORMS PART OF UK DOMESTIC LAW BY VIRTUE OF THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018 ("MAR").
14 January 2026
Mirriad Advertising plc
("
Mirriad
" or the "
Company
")
Full year trading update
Mirriad
, a leading provider of virtual product placement ("VPP"), provides an update on trading for the year ended 31 December 2025 and the outlook for 2026.
Subject to completion of the audit, Mirriad's revenue for 2025 is expected to total just over £0.4m (FY24: £1.0m).
Although Q4 sales were disappointing, the Board remains cautiously optimistic about Mirriad's outlook. The Company continues to work closely with its key partners, including Rembrand, and expectations are for a significantly stronger sales performance in 2026. The quieter than expected Q4 followed a broader industry-wide underperformance in media sales during 2025, which affected partners' full suite of products across the sector.
In parallel, the Company has entered discussions with strategic partners in a number of key emerging markets to establish joint ventures. These discussions are aimed at capitalising on Mirriad's significantly reduced campaign delivery costs, enabled by further automation and enhanced scalability. Negotiations are underway at varying stages with potential partners in a number of regions, including India, Indonesia, the Philippines, Thailand and Malaysia, with some being structured around meaningful minimum guarantees. Expansion into the Middle East has progressed well and contributed significantly to revenues in H2.
The Company is also in discussion with a major UK media company to take to market a test campaign and is progressing discussions towards an agreement. In alignment with the indication received from certain partners, the Board anticipates a potentially significant uplift in sales during the upcoming February-March period.
The Company had cash at 31 December 2025 of £1.2 million. This included the £350k R&D tax credit received in December. Costs continue to be carefully managed and, as announced on 19 December 2025, the current cost base is c. £220k per month.
For further information please visit
www.mirriad.com
or contact:
Mirriad Advertising plc
Louis Wakefield, Chief Executive Officer

latest.content:
19 Dec 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 2050M
Mirriad Advertising PLC
19 December 2025
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF ARTICLE 7 OF THE MARKET ABUSE REGULATION (596/2014/EU) AS IT FORMS PART OF UK DOMESTIC LAW BY VIRTUE OF THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018 ("MAR").
19 December 2025
Mirriad Advertising plc
("
Mirriad
" or the "
Company
")
Trading update
Mirriad, a leading provider of virtual product placement ("
VPP
") provides an update on trading for the year ending 31 December 2025.
Revenue for the second half of the year ("
H2
") is expected to be approximately £200k, with some final revenue still expected to be recognised before the end of December. As a result, full year revenue is expected to be approximately £0.4m. The vast majority of revenue in H2 to date has been derived from ROW outside of the US.
Cash at 30 November 2025 was approximately £1m and the Company anticipates receiving a tax credit of c. £350k shortly. As announced in the interim results, the Company's cost base has been significantly reduced and is currently approximately £220k per month.
Mirriad will provide a full year trading update at the beginning of January.
For further information please visit

company_30s.what_they_do: Mirriad builds software that lets brands embed ads inside movies, TV shows, and other video content.  Its platform uses AI to match ads with the right moments, making the experience feel natural rather than intrusive.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global video‑ad market is growing fast, and advertisers are looking for ways to reach viewers without interrupting them.  Mirriad’s technology offers a unique, scalable solution that can be used across streaming services, social media and even in‑app video, giving it a competitive edge in a large, expanding market.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in a very early growth phase, with limited revenue and a heavy focus on raising capital and building partnerships.  Its share price is trading near the lower end of its 52‑week range, signalling a steep decline from previous highs.

rns_narrative: Mirriad has been busy restructuring its board and senior management, and it recently announced a new joint‑venture to accelerate product roll‑out.  These moves suggest the company is trying to shore up its capital base and bring new revenue streams online.

The latest trading update and interim results indicate that the company is still burning cash, but it has secured an R&D tax credit and a potential £1.5m funding round that could give it breathing room for another 12‑18 months.
Overall, the narrative is one of survival and incremental partnership activity, rather than a sudden commercial breakthrough.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Skeptical but watching for a lifeline
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders see the JV and the funding announcement as a sign that Mirriad’s AI ad platform could finally hit the market, but they note that the company still needs a clear revenue story.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Many voices point to weak governance, frequent board changes, and a history of missing targets.  They fear the company may run out of cash before it can turn the technology into profit.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from outright criticism of management to a cautious ‘wait‑and‑see’ stance after the recent partnership news, but overall optimism remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for Mirriad is flat – no recent search spikes, meaning the market is largely unaware and a surprise catalyst could hit hard.

near_term_catalysts: The announcement of the joint venture in the next 30‑45 days could open a new distribution channel for the platform.
If the company secures the £1.5m funding round, it will have enough runway to push product launches and generate early revenue.
A significant uptick in trading volume that breaks the current low‑volume plateau would signal that institutional buyers are starting to accumulate.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a clear sign that the board has finalized the JV terms, a formal funding announcement, or a sudden spike in volume that exceeds the current daily average by 30‑50%.  Any of these would confirm that the deep crash is moving into the recovery phase.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock sits in the late‑stage of a deep‑crash cycle: capitulation appears complete, but mean‑reversion is still pending.  A tight APEX score indicates the price is under compression and ready to spring if a catalyst materialises.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Mirriad is a 70‑95% drop, which historically yields high upside when a catalyst arrives.  If the stock recovers to about halfway of its 52‑week high, the upside could be roughly 100% from current levels.  The recent JV and potential funding are the key drivers that could trigger this bounce, and the absence of active negative sentiment makes the market a good place to wait for surprise.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company’s cash burn remains high and its revenue pipeline is still thin.  If the funding round falls through or the JV fails to deliver, the share could fall to zero.  The worst‑case scenario is a continued decline to a price floor near zero, with no clear path to profitability.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 2x to 3x the current price if the JV or funding materialises.  Potential downside: loss of the entire invested capital if the company fails to secure cash or generate revenue.  The asymmetry is clear – a single successful catalyst could produce a massive return, but failure would wipe out the stake.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when volume spikes above the 30‑day average and the price touches the lower support level identified by the APEX compression zone.  Allocate 10–15% of the trading account to this position and set a stop 5% below the entry to limit loss to a single digit of the current price.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a formal funding announcement or a clear JV agreement.  If the share price stays below the 52‑week low for 60 days with no volume rebound, consider tightening the stop or reducing exposure.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy, if a delisting notice is issued, or if the board resigns en masse, exit immediately and cut losses.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Mirriad is a deep‑crashed play with a clear survival narrative; if the upcoming partnership or funding materialises, the upside is huge compared to the all‑in risk.

panic_analysis.interpretation: 🟠 Fear dominant - watch for reversal signs
panic_analysis.opportunity_reason: ⚠️ HIGH PANIC + Early timing = Potential accumulation phase
